title: Liongard Metric Primitive
description: Schema for a single entry in primitives/metrics-registry.yaml. Each primitive is an atomic, reusable evaluation against Liongard data — referenced by recipes via its stable @id so all downstream artifacts stay in sync when the primitive is revised.

name.description: Human-readable label for the primitive — used in recipe rendering and error messages.

description.description: What the primitive answers in one sentence. Used by the recipe-picker skill to match user intent.

evaluates.description: The inspector this primitive evaluates against.

inspectorSlug.description: Kebab-case inspector identifier from reference/inspector-aliases.md. Must end in '-inspector'.

inspectorId.description: Numeric inspector ID from internal/inspector-name-system-id-mapping.xlsx. May differ across instances — informational only.

jmesPath.description: Inline JMESPath expression evaluated against the inspector dataprint. Exactly one of: (a) jmesPath, (b) liongardMetricUuid, (c) liongardMetricName must be present.

liongardMetricUuid.description: CANONICAL cross-instance reference to a stored Liongard Global metric, once the UUID endpoint is available. Stable across every Liongard deployment.

liongardMetricName.description: Acceptable canonical reference until UUID is exposed. Must match the live liongard_metric LIST output exactly for User='Global' metrics — same name on every deployment. Do NOT source from Liongard-Metrics.xlsx (it's a snapshot).

liongardMetricId.description: Local per-instance numeric ID. NOT a stable reference — informational/optimization hint only. The validator warns when this is the only liongard* field present.

resultType.description: Shape of the EVALUATE result. Used by recipes to decide downstream handling (count, iterate, render).

resultUnit.description: Unit hint when resultType is integer. Pin this so threshold comparisons don't drift (catalog trap from learnings.md — hours vs. minutes vs. days).

compliantWhen.description: Logical condition under which the result indicates a compliant / healthy state. Free text but should be evaluable (e.g., 'result == 0', 'result >= 95', 'all entries have enable_status == "enabled"').

status.description: Validation status per CLAUDE.md Phase 4. VALIDATED = confirmed non-null on a live dataprint. SCHEMA_CONFIRMED = field exists but array empty / degraded on test system. PROPOSED = not yet confirmed; requires Liongard metric request.

validatedOn.description: Date the primitive was last validated against a live dataprint. Format YYYY-MM-DD.

validatedSystemReference.description: Anonymized reference to the system where validation ran (e.g., 'System A — dev environment'). NEVER real customer / tenant / partner names — see CLAUDE.md scrub rule.

answersQuestion.description: Cyber-insurance / CIS / onboarding QA questions this primitive provides evidence for. References by @id.

compliesWith.description: Compliance framework references this primitive maps to.

supersedes.description: @id of an older primitive this one replaces (e.g., a stored Liongard metric superseding an inline JMESPath workaround).

sourceRecipe.description: Path of the recipe this primitive was first extracted from. Provenance only — the validator builds the full reverse index from recipe primitives[] arrays.

fieldGotcha.description: Catalog-trap note when the field's shape or unit is non-obvious (per CLAUDE.md Phase 4 known traps). Surfaces in recipe rendering so MSP-side authors don't repeat the mistake.

anyOf.title: Primitive backed by a stored Liongard Global metric — UUID (preferred)
Primitive backed by a stored Liongard Global metric — Name (acceptable until UUID exposed)
